nodes.context: Colombian drug cartel based in Cali, Valle del Cauca, active 1975-1995, controlled 80% of world cocaine market at peak.
Colombian drug cartel led by Pablo Escobar, rival of Cali Cartel.
Italian criminal organization, allied with Cali Cartel for European cocaine distribution.
Vigilante group formed by drug cartels to combat kidnappings.
Colombian guerrilla group that kidnapped Marta Nieves Ochoa.
Colombian guerrilla faction that kidnapped Christina Santa Cruz.
Group formed to kill or capture Pablo Escobar, believed to include Cali Cartel members.
Mexican drug cartel, ally of Cali Cartel (defunct).
Group of drug traffickers opposing extradition, ally of Cali Cartel (defunct).
Mexican drug cartel, ally of Cali Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel, ally of Cali Cartel.
Colombian drug cartel, later became rivals of Cali Cartel (defunct).
Drug cartel based in southern Colombia, controlled over 80% of world's cocaine market at its peak.
Drug cartel based in Medellín, Colombia, led by Pablo Escobar.
Joint venture between Cali Cartel and Medellín Cartel to combat kidnappings.
Group formed by Cali Cartel to target Medellín Cartel and Pablo Escobar.

edges.evidence_quote: They broke away from Pablo Escobar and his Medellín associates in 1988
edges.context: Cali Cartel split from Medellín Cartel in 1988.

edges.evidence_quote: the Cali cartel established a strategic alliance with the powerful Camorra criminal organization. Cali supplied the cocaine and the Camorra handled distribution across Europe.
edges.context: Cali Cartel allied with Camorra for cocaine distribution in Europe.

edges.evidence_quote: the Medellín and Cali cartels, as well as associated traffickers, formed the group Muerte a Secuestradores (MAS; "Death to Kidnappers").
edges.context: Cali Cartel co-founded MAS with Medellín Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Herrera is believed to have been a founding member of Los Pepes
edges.context: Cali Cartel leader Herrera was a founding member of Los Pepes.
Cali Cartel listed Guadalajara Cartel as an ally.
Cali Cartel listed The Extraditables as an ally.
Cali Cartel listed Juarez Cartel as an ally.
Cali Cartel listed Gulf Cartel as an ally.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Norte del Valle Cartel (later became rivals) (defunct)
edges.context: Cali Cartel initially allied with Norte del Valle Cartel, later became rivals.
Cali Cartel listed Norte del Valle Cartel as a rival.
Cali Cartel listed Medellín Cartel as a rival.

edges.evidence_quote: The two cartels divided up the major United States distribution points: the Cali Cartel took New York City and the Medellín Cartel took South Florida and Miami; Los Angeles was left up for grabs.
edges.context: The cartels cooperated by dividing US distribution territories.

edges.evidence_quote: By 1987, the cooperation forged by the formation of MAS no longer existed. Contributing to the demise was the Medellín Cartel's Rodríguez Gacha, who attempted to move in on the New York City market, previously ceded to the Cali Cartel, and the 1986 arrest of Jorge Ochoa at a police roadblock, which the Medellín Cartel deemed suspicious and attributed partly to the Cali Cartel.
edges.context: The cartels became rivals as Medellín encroached on Cali's territory and blamed Cali for Ochoa's arrest.

edges.evidence_quote: The two cartels participated in other joint ventures in later years, such as the founding of Muerte a Secuestradores (MAS), who successfully returned Ochoa's kidnapped sister, Marta Nieves Ochoa.
edges.context: Cali Cartel co-founded MAS with Medellín Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The two cartels participated in other joint ventures in later years, such as the founding of Muerte a Secuestradores (MAS), who successfully returned Ochoa's kidnapped sister, Marta Nieves Ochoa.
edges.context: Medellín Cartel co-founded MAS with Cali Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: the Cali Cartel grew in strength, eventually founding Los Pepes, or Perseguidos por Pablo Escobar ("People Persecuted by Pablo Escobar"). Los Pepes was specifically formed to target the Medellín Cartel and bring about the downfall of Pablo Escobar.
edges.context: Cali Cartel founded Los Pepes to target Medellín Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Pepes was specifically formed to target the Medellín Cartel and bring about the downfall of Pablo Escobar.
edges.context: Los Pepes was created to fight the Medellín Cartel.
